resolution_note: Operator manually rotates the carousel and pulls each squeegee stroke. The manual anchor; no motor or indexer. Screen printing's defining axis - no inkjet category has a Manual value.
Ink forced through a stencilled mesh screen by a squeegee - one screen per colour. No printhead, no dpi.
Colours = number of screens/print heads. Distinct from inkjet ink channels - each colour is a separate stencilled screen.

original_value: automatic screen printing press, servo indexer
automatic press, AC servo + chain drive
cycles up to 1,200 pieces per hour
resolution_note: Screen presses rate speed in prints (pieces)/hour, not m2/hr - one garment per index cycle.

value: Dual chain-drive + AC servo; independent print-head lift

resolution_note: Optional hybrid module adds inkjet digital to the screen press - a bridge to the digital categories.
Oversize/all-over capability - the largest image area in the pilot, for cut-piece and all-over garment printing.
M&R's Ink Dip keeps ink in the print area between strokes - a screen-specific mechanism with no inkjet analogue.
